source: ECI — Voter Helplines, Contacts and Key Resources

chunks.heading: Key voter helplines and official contacts
chunks.text:
VOTER HELPLINES AND OFFICIAL CONTACTS FOR INDIAN VOTERS:

NATIONAL VOTER HELPLINE: Call 1950 (toll-free, available in all states and UTs). Services include name search, form guidance, grievance registration, and booth information. Available throughout the year with extended hours during election season.

VOTER HELPLINE APP: Download from Google Play Store or Apple App Store (search Voter Helpline). Services: All voter registration forms, name search in electoral roll, e-EPIC download, booth locator, grievance filing, application tracking.

cVIGIL APP: For reporting election code violations. Download from Play Store or App Store. Active only during the election period after Model Code of Conduct enforcement. The ECI targets 100-minute response time for complaints.

NATIONAL VOTERS SERVICE PORTAL: https://voters.eci.gov.in — all voter registration services online including Form 6, Form 8, e-EPIC download, application tracking.

ELECTORAL SEARCH PORTAL: https://electoralsearch.eci.gov.in — check your name in the electoral roll.

ECI OFFICIAL WEBSITE: https://eci.gov.in — election notifications, press releases, all official forms and handbooks.

SMS SERVICE: Send your EPIC number to 1950 to receive voter details via SMS.

SAKSHAM APP: For PwD voters — request home voting, transport to booth, wheelchair facility, and find accessible polling stations.

chunks.heading: What is SVEEP — voter education programme?
chunks.text:
Q: What is SVEEP and what does the Election Commission do to educate voters?

A: SVEEP stands for Systematic Voters Education and Electoral Participation. It is the ECI flagship programme for voter education and outreach.

Objective: To ensure maximum, ethical, and informed participation of all eligible voters.

Key SVEEP activities:
1. Campus Ambassador Programme: Student volunteers in colleges spread voter awareness among youth.
2. Voter Awareness Forums: Community groups, RWAs, NGOs organize awareness drives.
3. Icon-based outreach: ECI appoints National and State Icons (celebrities from films, sports, arts) to encourage registration.
4. Nukkad Natak (street plays): Voter education through folk performances in rural areas.
5. Social media campaigns: Mass campaigns on Facebook, Instagram, X targeting urban youth.
6. Accessible content: Materials in sign language, audio format, and regional languages.
7. Electoral Literacy Clubs (ELCs): Voter awareness built into school curricula.

Voter Helpline 1950 is the primary contact point for all voter queries throughout India.

chunks.heading: What is cVIGIL and how to report election violations?
chunks.text:
Q: What is cVIGIL App and how can I report election violations?

A: cVIGIL (Citizen Vigilance) is the ECI mobile app for citizens to report election code violations in real time.

What can be reported:
- Distribution of cash, liquor, or gifts to voters (voter bribery)
- Unauthorized campaign material display (banners, flags, posters)
- Use of vehicles for voter transportation by parties
- Hate speech or inflammatory statements
- Use of government resources for campaigning
- Any Model Code of Conduct violation

How it works:
1. Download cVIGIL from Play Store or App Store
2. Click a photo or short video (max 2 minutes) of the violation
3. Submit — your GPS location is automatically captured
4. A flying squad is dispatched to your location
5. You receive a complaint status update

Anonymity: You can file complaints anonymously. Your details are not shared with the accused.

Availability: cVIGIL is ONLY active during election period after MCC enforcement. Not available between elections.

Response target: ECI targets 100-minute response time for all cVIGIL complaints.
